samples.xml_description:
<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Serial-to-Parallel Conversion Circuit</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data.</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data (8 bits wide).</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of clk or negative edge of rst_n.
        Inside this block, we check if rst_n is active (low). If so, we reset cnt to 0 and dout_parallel to 0.
        We also initialize dout_valid to 0.
        
        Combinational logic block triggered by any change in cnt or din_valid.
        In this block, we check if cnt has reached 8. If so, we set dout_parallel[cnt] to din_serial and increment cnt by 1.
        We then set dout_valid to 1 if cnt is 8, otherwise we set it to 0.
        
        Always block triggered on the positive edge of clk.
        This block checks if cnt is greater than 8. If so, we reset cnt to 0.
    </implementation>
</module>

                                       : ... In instance serial2parallel

                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTH */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.

      |         ^~~
